key_insight: Adaptive Mamba Neural Operators replace the usual kernel-integral view of state-space models with reproducing-kernel representations built from Takenaka–Malmquist systems, enabling flexible PDE solution learning on varied geometries and meshes.
engineering_relevance: This offers a potentially more robust neural-operator framework for simulation tasks in fluids, solids, and finance where data come from point clouds, structured grids, or irregular domains.
method: Construct reproducing kernels for state-space models instead of using the standard SSM integral kernel.
Build Takenaka–Malmquist systems to match PDE solution structure and geometry.
Leverage adaptive Fourier decomposition theory to form an explainable operator representation.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports benchmark testing on challenging PDE problems across fluid physics, solid physics, and finance, with AMO consistently achieving lower relative L2 error than state-of-the-art solvers.
watch_item: Performance gains are reported from the abstract only; practical deployment may depend on stable construction of the adaptive kernel basis and good generalization across unseen geometries.
